model_summary: Initial observation model for postpartum emotional distress focusing on eight operationalized criteria (identity change, bonding, entrapment, guilt, overwhelm, tearfulness, self-criticism, perceived invalidation). Predictors prioritized to be modifiable and measurable at the EMA / daily / passive timescales: sleep (subjective + passive), self-compassion practice, cognitive reappraisal use, perceived social validation, respite hours, physical activity (steps), and social-media/screen exposure. Design emphasizes within-person variance (1–9 Likert where possible), coherent sampling (6 EMA prompts/day for momentary states, 1 daily morning diary for sleep/trait items, passive daily/ per-prompt aggregation), and parsimony to support later gVAR estimation while preserving coverage of BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers.
variable_selection_notes: Selected criteria preserve full complaint resolution (all 8 original criterion variables retained). Predictor selection prioritized high HyDe/LLM signals and mechanistic plausibility: sleep (top-ranked in LLM mappings), self-compassion/emotion-regulation (HyDe top-ranked), cognitive reappraisal/behavioral activation (CBT signals), social support/respite (SOCIAL signals), activity and passive digital exposures as contextual modifiers. Trade-offs: limited number of overlapping constructs to reduce collinearity (chose one broad self-compassion measure and one cognitive-reappraisal measure rather than multiple overlapping self-regulation items). Passive signals chosen to increase objective variance but are optional if device access is lacking. Uncertainty/bias notes: identity-change (C01) is more trait-like (low within-person variability) and will likely require detrending or differencing; perceived bonding (C02) shows ceiling risk; screen-time effects are potentially nonlinear, so we model them as such rather than forcing monotonic signs.

momentary_ema.notes: Fixed 6 blocks with small random jitter to reduce predictability and memory biases. Use single-screen short EMA (<30s) per prompt to minimize burden.

daily_diary.notes: Morning diary (07:30) capturing last-night sleep subjective rating and previous day's respite hours / summary of major events. Use brief structured items.

passive_streams.aggregation_strategy: passive_continuous then aggregate per-prompt and daily
passive_streams.notes: Collect continuous phone/wearable-derived steps and screen-time and sleep-tracking where available. Aggregate steps and screen-time into 'since last prompt' windows for per-prompt alignment; sleep minutes aggregated nightly for morning diary.

design_recommendations.gvar_readiness_notes: Expect ~168 momentary + 28 daily observations; after preprocessing (imputation, detrending, z-scoring), gVAR with this individual time series is feasible. Control for nonstationarity (C01 low within-person variance) by detrending or treating C01 as slow-moving exogenous or using difference series. Use person-mean centering for predictors and criteria before VAR modeling.
design_recommendations.variance_support_notes: Use 1–9 Likert scales for subjective constructs to increase variance. Passive step/screen aggregates will add continuous variability. Encourage wearing phone/wearable and enable OS sleep tracking to support objective sleep minutes.
design_recommendations.missingness_risk_notes: EMA burden (6/day) may induce missingness; implement brief prompts, user reminders, and allow Grace windows. Passive streams mitigate missingness but depend on permissions and device availability; collect meta-data on missingness and device status.

criteria_variables.label: Altered sense of self since childbirth

measurement.item_or_signal: Thinking about the period since having your baby, how much do you feel like 'yourself' compared to before the baby? (1 = completely like myself, 9 = not at all like myself)
measurement.notes: Trait-like; expect slow changes across weeks. Recommended to model as slow-moving or difference series in gVAR.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering and detrend (first-difference or low-pass filter); consider modeling as exogenous slow variable

criteria_variables.label: Positive emotional bond with the baby

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how strongly did you feel affectionate/connected to your baby? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Ceiling risk possible because 'love' is present; measure intensity to preserve variance.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you feel trapped or limited by your caregiving role? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Momentary fluctuations expected (respite, help, naps may alter).

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider logit transform if bounded clustering

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how guilty did you feel about your feelings or parenting? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Direct target of self-compassion and cognitive reappraisal interventions.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how overwhelmed or unable to cope did you feel? (1 = not at all, 9 = completely overwhelmed)
measurement.notes: High expected within-person variance; sensitive to sleep, respite, and coping skills.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how tearful or emotionally reactive were you to small triggers? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Responsive to sleep, self-regulation skills, and short-term supports.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: person-mean centering; consider square-root for skewed distributions

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, after feeling upset, how much did you judge yourself negatively (e.g., 'bad parent')? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Direct target for compassion-focused and cognitive reappraisal interventions.

criteria_variables.label: Perceived minimization / lack of validation from others

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, to what extent did you feel that others dismissed or minimized how you feel? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Important social-context variable; may moderate responsiveness to interventions.

predictor_variables.label: Subjective sleep quality (last night)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Poor sleep quality increases emotional reactivity, low coping capacity, and tearfulness; improving sleep can rapidly reduce negative affect.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Establish consistent sleep window, optimize sleep environment (dark, cool), brief sleep hygiene routines; discuss with clinician if persistent.

measurement.item_or_signal: Last night, how well did you sleep overall? (1 = very poorly, 9 = very well)
measurement.notes: Morning diary item aligned with passive sleep minutes; subjectivity captures restfulness.

expected_effects.rationale: Improved sleep over nights gradually supports restored sense of self; lag may be days.
Feeling rested may modestly strengthen positive affect toward baby.
Poor sleep increases perceived entrapment via reduced coping capacity.
Better sleep reduces negative affect and guilt intensity.
Sleep has strong immediate effects on subjective overwhelm and coping.
Sleep improves emotional reactivity and reduces tearfulness the next day.
Sustained better sleep reduces negative self-evaluation over days.
Indirect effect: better sleep increases capacity to tolerate invalidation.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Sleep items are low risk; if severe insomnia suspected, recommend clinical follow-up.

feasibility.notes: Morning diary is low burden; high compliance expected.

predictor_variables.label: Objective sleep minutes (night)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Total sleep minutes objectively index recovery; shorter sleep is linked to worse mood and coping.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Align sleep schedule; obtain wearable/phone-based sleep tracking; discuss clinical options if severely short.

measurement.channel: phone/wearable sleep tracking (OS/native app or connected wearable)
measurement.notes: Requires user permission for sleep data; fallbacks to self-report if unavailable.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p for extreme tails; person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Shorter sleep linked to greater next-day overwhelm.
Objective sleep loss increases emotional reactivity.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Passive collection requires consent; be transparent about data use.

feasibility.notes: Feasible if user grants permissions and has device; otherwise missingness likely.

predictor_variables.label: Self-compassion practice / self-soothing (use intensity)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Self-compassion practices directly reduce guilt and self-criticism and improve emotion regulation.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Brief self-compassion scripts, compassionate phrasing, short guided audio (1-5 minutes).

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, when you were upset, how compassionate were you toward yourself? (1 = not at all, 9 = extremely)
measurement.notes: Directly targets guilt and self-criticism mechanisms highlighted in the complaint.

expected_effects.rationale: Self-compassion should rapidly reduce guilt and shame-related reactions.
Direct reduction in self-criticism following compassionate self-talk.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Low risk; ensure practices are brief and grounding.

feasibility.notes: EMA items short and tied to prompts; training examples can be provided.

predictor_variables.label: Cognitive reappraisal / thought reframing frequency
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Reappraisal reduces guilt and self-blame by changing appraisal of events; supports identity updating over time.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short reappraisal prompts: 'What evidence supports/against that thought?', cognitive cards; guided reframes.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how often did you try to reframe or challenge negative thoughts? (1 = not at all, 9 = very often)

expected_effects.rationale: Reappraisal reduces immediate self-critical thoughts after emotional episodes.
Reframing reduces guilt intensity over short timescales.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Cognitive strategies are low risk; encourage professional support if intrusive thoughts escalate.

predictor_variables.label: Perceived social support / validation
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Perceived validation reduces feelings of invalidation and isolation and can moderate guilt and entrapment.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short validation scripts, brief supportive check-ins, scheduling a trusted person to listen.

measurement.item_or_signal: Since the last prompt, how supported or understood did you feel by others? (1 = not at all, 9 = completely supported)
measurement.notes: Captures social-context fluctuations; targets perceived minimization (C08) directly.

expected_effects.rationale: Immediate reductions in perceived invalidation following support/validation.
Perceived support alleviates feelings of entrapment by enabling resource sharing.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Asking about social support may surface relationship issues; provide referral options if distress increases.

feasibility.notes: Simple EMA item; likely high compliance.

predictor_variables.label: Respite / childcare hours (available help)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Objective access to respite directly relieves overwhelm and perceived entrapment and yields immediate improvements in coping.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Scheduling short breaks, arranging family/friend or paid childcare, micro-respite planning.

measurement.item_or_signal: Yesterday, how many hours of childcare/respite (by others) did you receive? (enter number of hours)
measurement.notes: Captured in morning diary as sum for previous day; can be sensitive; anonymize if storing free-text.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p for skewed hours; person-mean centering

expected_effects.rationale: Respite has immediate effect on perceived entrapment and overwhelm.
Respite increases coping capacity quickly.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Asking about childcare access may reveal lack of resources; provide resources/referral options.

feasibility.notes: Daily reporting reduces recall bias vs. momentary.

predictor_variables.label: Physical activity (steps per prompt-window)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Light activity influences mood and fatigue and can reduce tearfulness and overwhelm over short timescales.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Short walks, paced activity during baby naps.

measurement.item_or_signal: Steps recorded in the window since last prompt (device-derived)
measurement.notes: Aggregate into per-prompt windows to align with EMA.

expected_effects.rationale: Short activity bursts can reduce immediate emotional reactivity in some individuals.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Passive step data requires permission; may be missing if phone not carried.

feasibility.notes: Useful objective covariate but optional if devices unavailable.

predictor_variables.label: Screen time / social media minutes (since last prompt)
predictor_variables.mechanism_rationale: Digital exposure/social media may have nonlinear effects on mood — short social connection vs. longer passive browsing increasing guilt/comparison.
predictor_variables.intervention_examples_safe: Time-limited social-media use, curated content, brief social-media breaks.

measurement.channel: phone screen-time API (minutes per app or aggregated)
measurement.item_or_signal: Minutes of screen time (or social-media app usage) since last prompt
measurement.notes: Expect nonlinear associations; measure continuously and test for U-shaped patterns.

gvar_handling.suggested_transformation: log1p; also consider quadratic terms or splines in modeling

expected_effects.rationale: Short social contact may reduce tearfulness, while prolonged passive browsing may increase negative comparisons and tearfulness.

predictor_variables.risk_ethics_notes: Privacy-sensitive; obtain explicit consent and explain aggregation; do not collect content.

feasibility.notes: Depends on OS permissions and app-level aggregation availability.

edges.notes: Better subjective sleep last night expected to reduce next-day overwhelm.
Self-compassion practice expected to reduce guilt quickly.
Additional respite hours directly lower entrapment and overwhelm.
Activity bursts may reduce tearfulness within hours.
Screen time may have U-shaped effects on tearfulness (short contact vs prolonged passive browsing).

expected_collinearity_risks: P03 (self-compassion) vs P04 (reappraisal): moderate collinearity of adaptive emotion-regulation skill use.
P01 (subjective sleep quality) vs P02 (objective sleep minutes): high collinearity expected; choose single or orthogonalize if necessary.
expected_floor_ceiling_risks: C02 (positive bond) may show ceiling effects (responses clustered toward high values); consider modeling limited variance.
P06 (respite hours) may be zero-inflated leading to floor effects on many days.
variance_enhancement_suggestions: Use 1-9 Likert anchored scales (already applied) to increase within-person variance.
Encourage passive data capture to add continuous variance (steps, screen-time).
If C01 shows too little variance, treat as slow-moving covariate or collect weekly identity-reflection items.
excluded_or_deprioritized_candidates: Pharmacotherapy and invasive neuromodulation (e.g., TMS) deprioritized for initial within-person gVAR exploration due to complexity and need for clinical oversight.
Highly overlapping self-regulation subscales were avoided to reduce collinearity (kept one self-compassion and one cognitive reappraisal measure).
diagnostics.gvar_feasibility_summary: With 28 days, 6 EMA prompts/day (168 momentary points) + 28 daily diaries and passive streams, there is sufficient temporal resolution for single-subject gVAR with 8 criterion and 8 predictor nodes after preprocessing. Recommend person-mean centering, detrending for slow-moving variables, and handling missingness with interpolation or multiple imputation tailored to time-series.

safety_notes: No immediate acute self-harm or psychosis signals in the complaint text. Monitor responses for emergent safety risk (explicit suicidal ideation, intent, or severe functioning loss); if reported, encourage immediate contact with local emergency services or a clinician. For questions about resource scarcity (childcare), provide referral options. Passive data collection requires explicit consent and clear privacy explanations.
